data.description: This message returns router tally information in response to an EXTENDED CROSSPOINT INTERROGATE message (Command Byte 0x81).

This message is issued spontaneously by the controller on all ports after it has confirmation that a route has been made through the router.
The message is effectively broadcast.

This message is issued by a controller in response to an EXTENDED PROTECT INTERROGATE message (Command Byte 0x8a).
It returns the current protect status of a destination.

This message is issued by the controller in when the protect data is altered and also if the data was unsuccessfully altered as a result of an EXTENDED PROTECT CONNECT message (Command Bytes 0x8c).
This message is broadcast on all ports.
NOTE:
Following the issue of an extended protect connected message SYSTEM3 will then issue a PROTECT DEVICE NAME REQUEST message (Command Byte 0x11).
The remote device should then respond with a PROTECT DEVICE NAME RESPONSE message (Command Byte 0x12).

This command is issued by the controller when the protect data is altered i.e. a destination has been unprotected and also if the data was unsuccessfully altered as a result of an EXTENDED PROTECT DIS-CONNECT message (Command Bytes 0x8e)
This message is broadcast on all ports.

This message is issued by a controller in response to an EXTENDED CROSSPOINT TALLY DUMP REQUEST (Command Byte 0x95). 
It provides tally table data for a given matrix/level combination.
This message assumes a maximum destination/source number of 65535.The message length is limited in total to 133 bytes, thus, tables that would exceed this length would cause more than one message
to be issued with the appropriate destination value in the bytes 4 and 5 of the message.

This message is issued by a controller in response to an EXTENDED CROSSPOINT TALLY DUMP REQUEST (Command Byte 0x95). 
It provides tally table data for a given matrix/level combination.
This message assumes a maximum destination/source number of 65535.The message length is limited in total to 133 bytes, thus, tables that would exceed this length would cause more than one message
to be issued with the appropriate destination value in the bytes 4 and 5 of the message.

This command is issued by the controller in response to EXTENDED ALL SOURCE NAMES REQUEST and EXTENDED SINGLE SOURCE NAME REQUEST messages (Command Bytes 0xe4 and 0xe5).
This message is broadcast on all ports.

This command is issued by the controller in response to an EXTENDED ALL DESTINATION ASSOCIATION NAMES REQUEST or EXTENDED SINGLE DESTINATION ASSOCIATION NAME REQUEST messages (Command Bytes 0xe6 and 0xe7).
The message length restrictions in 3.5.10 apply.

This message is issued by the controller in response to an EXTENDED  CROSSPOINT CONNECT ON GO GROUP SALVO message (Command Byte 0xf8).
'N.B.: The group salvo commands are only implemented on the XD and ECLIPSE router ranges

This message is issued by the controller in response to a CROSSPOINT GROUP SALVO INTERROGATE message (Command Byte 0x7c).
It returns the SALVO data for the specified SALVO GROUP.
The data returned is for a particular index, the validity flag indicates whether any more data is present.
'N.B.: The group salvo commands are only implemented on the XD and ECLIPSE router ranges
